title: Tapping for Turning 50
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for the complicated feelings of a significant birthday
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice for the milestone birthday that hits harder than expected. Addresses the grief, the stock-taking, and the things you thought you would have done by now, alongside what is actually here.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the weeks around a significant birthday — 50, 55, 60 — when the milestone is raising more feeling than you expected.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for the milestone birthday that arrived with more feeling than anticipated. Use it when the occasion is bringing up grief, stock-taking, or the gap between where you are and where you thought you would be.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I am turning 50 and I did not expect to feel this complicated about it, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though there is grief here alongside whatever else is here, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am not where I thought I would be at this age, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I did not expect this birthday to hit this hard.
**Side of eye:** There is grief here alongside everything else.
**Under eye:** There are things I thought I would have done by now.
**Under nose:** And things I have done that I did not expect.
**Chin:** This age feels bigger than I thought it would.
**Collarbone:** I am allowed to feel both the loss and the arrival.
**Under arm:** I don't have to make this simple.
**Top of head:** This is a big moment. I am in it.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I have arrived here. That is something.
**Side of eye:** I know things at 50 that I could not have known at 30.
**Under eye:** The decade ahead is mine to set.
**Under nose:** I have more resource in me than I have sometimes known what to do with.
**Chin:** This age is not a penalty.
**Collarbone:** I am choosing what this means.
**Under arm:** There is time still. The shape of it has changed. It is still time.
**Top of head:** I am here. I am still building. This counts.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
